content.divergence_summary: Same rain, entirely different relationship to it. Main has no buffer — rain closed the only viable west route and pushed the east offer from background to foreground. Alt1 had the hazelnut and the shade corridor map: rain became a reading opportunity, not a crisis.
content.branch_a_path: Main sheltered under the root cross with near-zero yield. Food at 0.21 — critical. The west is gone for now. The east offer reorganized from secondary to primary goal under pressure.
content.branch_b_path: Alt1 chose to observe rather than forage. The shade corridor drained east, confirming it as a watershed line aligned with the trade channel. Food at 0.52 with hazelnut in reserve. No urgency.
key_differences: Main's food is critical (0.21); alt1 has margin (0.52) and still holds the hazelnut
Main's primary goal is now resolve_east_offer; alt1 is still on map_west_territory
Rain was a crisis for main, an information source for alt1 — the difference is entirely buffer
Main is being pushed east by circumstance; alt1 is choosing to stay west to finish the map
Alt1's territory knowledge now has a hydrological layer; main's knowledge is unchanged
shared_elements: Both sheltered rather than forcing a full forage run
Both had attention increase — crisis focus for main, observational focus for alt1
Both experienced rain as a significant constraint on movement
Both have east on their radar, for different reasons
